message:
🐛 Fix settings sidebar wrong section highlighted on nav click (#2253)

The IntersectionObserver was overriding the activeSection set by
handleNavClick during smooth scroll animation, causing the wrong
sidebar item to be highlighted (e.g. clicking "Theme" highlighted
"Notifications" instead).

Root causes:
1. Observer callback continued processing entries during programmatic
   scrolls, then picked the first visible section in document order
   which might be sections passing through during scroll animation
2. When the observer was suppressed (return early), the visibleSections
   map became stale because entries were not processed — after
   suppression ended, stale data caused wrong highlights
3. The deep linking effect fired redundantly after handleNavClick's
   navigate() call, causing double-scroll conflicts

Fix:
- Add isNavScrollingRef to suppress observer's setActiveSection during
  programmatic scrolls (1200ms covers long smooth scroll animations)
- Always process observer entries to keep visibleSections accurate,
  but skip setActiveSection when suppressed
- Guard deep linking effect to skip when handleNavClick already
  scrolled (isNavScrollingRef check at top of effect)
- Wrap scrollToSection in useCallback for effect dependency correctness

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
